title: my press release for new CFO at HBL should be rewritten as more newsworthy news: Parul Das Joins as New CFO of HBL Bangl…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
my press release for new CFO at HBL should be rewritten as more newsworthy news: Parul Das Joins as New CFO of HBL Bangladesh 

Dhaka, Bangladesh, June 11, 2023: HBL Bangladesh is delighted to announce the appointment of Parul Das as its new Chief Financial Officer (CFO). Ms. Parul brings with her 23 years of extensive professional experience and a diverse background in the banking sector.
Parul Das started her career with Bangladesh Bank before moving on to the private sector, holding senior positions at renowned organizations such as City Bank, BRAC Bank, ONE Bank, BD Finance, Hoda Vasi Chowdhury & Co. Chartered Accountants, and KPMG Bangladesh. Her roles included financial operations, business finance, capital management, project management, and various operational functions. Before joining HBL, she was the CFO of ONE Bank.

Ms. Parul earned her master’s and bachelor’s degrees in business administration from the University of Chittagong. She is a Fellow Member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Bangladesh (ICAB) and an Associate Member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of England & Wales (ICAEW).

Ms. Parul’s vast experience and strong financial acumen will enable HBL to further expand its business operations and strengthen HBL’s presence in the Bangladesh market, while supporting its regional and international growth. As CFO, she will be responsible for leading HBL’s financial strategy and ensuring continued growth across the Bangladeshi market.

HBL is proud to welcome Parul Das to its team, which is committed to serving customers across Bangladesh with innovative and customer-centric banking services to enhance HBL’s legacy of more than 45 years in the market. The addition of Das as CFO further solidifies HBL’s commitment to its growth and development in Bangladesh and the broader region.

An example from Uber: Uber appoints Prabhjeet Singh President of India, South Asia 

Dhaka, July 16, 2020: Uber today announced the appointment of Prabhjeet Singh as President Uber India and South Asia, tasked with overseeing the next phase of growth in the company’s  mobility business and ensuring safety for riders and drivers across India, Sri Lanka and Bangladesh. His appointment is effective immediately. 

While making the announcement Pradeep Parameswaran, Regional General Manager for Asia Pacific, said, “I’m delighted to announce that Prabh is  replacing me as Uber’s new President for India and South Asia, one of our fastest growing and most strategic markets. Prabh is a passionate and innovative leader and has been instrumental in helping build Uber from the ground up and established our category leadership in the ride-sharing market. I’m confident Prabh will exceed our expectations by leading Uber India SA on to the path of profitability, further consolidate our partnership with public transport authorities, continue our growth trajectory by expanding Auto and Moto to the next batch of cities, and build iconic teams.’’

Commenting on his new appointment Prabhjeet, said, “I’m thrilled to have been given the responsibility of leading Uber in India and South Asia, and look forward to collaborating with exceptional teams and gifted colleagues across the Uber family to strengthen our services and product offerings. Uber is an integral part of the fabric of our cities and as they start moving again, we have prioritized the highest standards of safety, sustainability and service that our riders and drivers expect of us.’’

Prabh, an IIT Kharagpur and IIM-A alum, joined Uber in August 2015 from McKinsey and Co., where he was an Associate Partner. Since, he’s been part of the core team that adapted the global business model to India, launched multiple new cities and led several India-first innovations, including scaling up Auto and Moto categories and building a multi-modal platform, which have both been exported to other emerging markets. 

Prabh will be supported in his new role by a seasoned regional leadership team. Key members include Joyjyoti Misra, former Partner at Khaitan & Co. now Director for Legal Affairs; Satinder Bindra, former Director United Nations and Principal Director Asian Development Bank, now Director Communications; Pavan Vaish, former co-founder and CEO of IBM Daksh, currently head of central operations; Rajiv Aggarwal, former Joint Secretary, Department of Industrial Policy and Promotion, presently Director Policy; Shirish Andhare, former Senior VP at  Ezetap Mobile Solutions, who heads Product at Uber; Neha Mathur, former GE executive, now head of HR; Priyanshu Singh, former COO, Lithium Urban Technologies, now head of Community Operation and Sanjay Gupta, former Urban Ladder and Marico executive, who’ll now have an expanded role as Marketing Director India and APAC Rides Brand Marketing. 

In keeping with Uber India South Asia’s tradition of being a reservoir of talent, other key South Asian leaders have recently been elevated to wider, regional roles. Former India SA President, Pradeep Parameswaran, is now the Regional General Manager APAC and Vidhya Duthaluru, former Director, Engineering, has also recently been promoted as the global head of engineering for the customer care platform.

HBL Bangladesh Welcomes Parul Das as New Chief Financial Officer

Dhaka, June 11, 2023: Parul Das has been appointed as the new Chief Financial Officer (CFO) of HBL Bangladesh, bringing 23 years of diverse experience in the banking sector. Previously, Ms. Das held senior positions at prestigious organizations, including Bangladesh Bank, City Bank, BRAC Bank, ONE Bank, BD Finance, Hoda Vasi Chowdhury & Co. Chartered Accountants, and KPMG Bangladesh.

A graduate of the University of Chittagong, Ms. Das is also a Fellow Member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Bangladesh (ICAB) and an Associate Member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of England & Wales (ICAEW). Her extensive expertise is expected to drive the growth of HBL's operations and solidify its presence in the Bangladesh market and beyond.

New President for Uber India and South Asia: Prabhjeet Singh Appointed

Dhaka, July 16, 2020: Uber has announced Prabhjeet Singh as the new President for Uber India and South Asia, responsible for the growth of the company's mobility business across India, Sri Lanka, and Bangladesh. As a leader in the ride-sharing market, Mr. Singh will focus on consolidating partnerships with public transport authorities and expanding Auto and Moto services in new cities.

An IIT Kharagpur and IIM-A graduate, Mr. Singh joined Uber in 2015 from McKinsey and Co. and has since played a significant role in adapting the global business model to India, launching new cities, and leading India-first innovations. His appointment follows the recent promotions of other key South Asian leaders in Uber India, demonstrating the company's commitment to fostering talent and growth within the region.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
HBL Bangladesh Appoints Parul Das as New CFO to Strengthen Local-International Network and Drive Future Growth

Dhaka, Bangladesh, June 11, 2023: HBL Bangladesh has announced the appointment of seasoned banking professional Parul Das as its new Chief Financial Officer (CFO). With 23 years of diverse experience in the industry, Ms. Das will play a central role in reinforcing HBL's presence in the Bangladesh market and driving its regional and international growth.

Ms. Das' illustrious career began at Bangladesh Bank and progressed to prominent organizations such as City Bank, BRAC Bank, ONE Bank, BD Finance, Hoda Vasi Chowdhury & Co. Chartered Accountants, and KPMG Bangladesh. She has a proven track record of excellence in financial operations, business finance, capital management, project management, and various operational functions. Prior to joining HBL, Ms. Das held the position of CFO at ONE Bank.

A proud graduate of the University of Chittagong, Ms. Das is a Fellow Member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Bangladesh (ICAB) and an Associate Member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of England & Wales (ICAEW). Her extensive expertise will be instrumental in expanding HBL's business operations and fostering collaboration through the China-Bangladesh window.

Upon her appointment, Ms. Das remarked, "I am honored and excited to join HBL Bangladesh as CFO. This is a unique and challenging opportunity to contribute to the growth and development of the bank in Bangladesh and the broader region. I look forward to working with the exceptional team at HBL to deliver value to our customers and stakeholders."

The Country Head of HBL Bangladesh also expressed enthusiasm for Ms. Das' appointment, stating, "We are delighted to welcome Parul Das to our team. Her strong financial acumen and industry experience will undoubtedly benefit HBL's growth trajectory and enhance our legacy of more than 45 years in the market. We are confident that Parul will lead our financial strategy effectively and help strengthen our local and international network."

With the addition of Ms. Das as CFO, HBL demonstrates its commitment to serving customers across Bangladesh with innovative and customer-centric banking services, solidifying its position as a leading player in the country's financial landscape.

HBL Bangladesh appoints new CFO Parul Das

Dhaka, Bangladesh, June 11, 2023: HBL, a bank of regional relevance with operations spread across multiple geographies, has announced the appointment of seasoned banking professional Parul Das as its new Chief Financial Officer (CFO) for Bangladesh. With 23 years of diverse experience in the industry, Parul Das will play a central role in reinforcing HBL’s presence in the Bangladesh market and driving its regional and international growth.
Ms. Das’ illustrious career began at Bangladesh Bank and progressed to prominent organizations such as City Bank, BRAC Bank, ONE Bank, BD Finance, Hoda Vasi Chowdhury & Co. Chartered Accountants, and KPMG Bangladesh. She has a proven track record of excellence in financial operations, business finance, capital management, project management, and various operational functions. Prior to joining HBL Bangladesh team, she held the position of CFO at ONE Bank.
While making the announcement Selim Barkat, Country Head of HBL Bangladesh, said, “We are delighted to welcome Parul Das to our team. Her strong financial acumen and industry experience will undoubtedly benefit HBL’s growth trajectory and enhance our legacy of more than 45 years in the market. We are confident that Parul will lead our financial strategy effectively and help strengthen our local and international network.”
Commenting on her appointment the new CFO of HBL Bangladesh Parul Das said, “I am honored and excited to join HBL Bangladesh as the Chief Financial Officer. This is a unique and challenging opportunity to contribute to the growth and development of the bank in Bangladesh and beyond. I look forward to working with the exceptional team at HBL to deliver value to our customers and stakeholders.”
A proud graduate of the University of Chittagong, Ms. Das is a Fellow Member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Bangladesh (ICAB) and an Associate Member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of England & Wales (ICAEW). Her extensive expertise will be instrumental in expanding HBL’s business operations locally and internationally, as well as fostering collaboration through the China-Bangladesh window.
With the addition of Parul Das as CFO, HBL demonstrates its commitment to serving customers across Bangladesh with innovative and customer-centric banking services, solidifying its position as a leading player in the country’s financial landscape.

HBL Bangladesh Appoints Parul Das as New CFO

Dhaka, Bangladesh, June 11, 2023: HBL, a bank with regional relevance and operations in multiple geographies, has announced the appointment of experienced banking professional Parul Das as its new Chief Financial Officer (CFO) for Bangladesh. Parul Das brings 23 years of diverse experience to her role, where she will help reinforce HBL's presence in the Bangladesh market and drive regional and international growth.

Ms. Das began her career at Bangladesh Bank and has since held positions in prominent organizations such as City Bank, BRAC Bank, ONE Bank, BD Finance, Hoda Vasi Chowdhury & Co. Chartered Accountants, and KPMG Bangladesh. She has a strong track record in financial operations, business finance, capital management, project management, and various operational functions. Prior to joining HBL Bangladesh, she was the CFO at ONE Bank.

In announcing the appointment, Selim Barkat, Country Head of HBL Bangladesh, said, "We are delighted to welcome Parul Das to our team. Her strong financial acumen and industry experience will undoubtedly benefit HBL's growth trajectory and enhance our more than 45-year legacy in the market. We are confident that Parul will lead our financial strategy effectively and help strengthen our local and international network."

The new CFO of HBL Bangladesh, Parul Das, commented, "I am honored and excited to join HBL Bangladesh as Chief Financial Officer. This is a unique and challenging opportunity to contribute to the growth and development of the bank in Bangladesh and beyond. I look forward to working with the exceptional team at HBL to deliver value to our customers and stakeholders."

Ms. Das, a graduate of the University of Chittagong, is a Fellow Member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of Bangladesh (ICAB) and an Associate Member of the Institute of Chartered Accountants of England & Wales (ICAEW). Her expertise will contribute to expanding HBL's business operations locally and internationally and fostering collaboration through the China-Bangladesh window.

With the appointment of Parul Das as CFO, HBL demonstrates its commitment to serving customers across Bangladesh with innovative, customer-centric banking services and solidifying its position as a leading player in the country's financial landscape.
